customer_anxiety_profile: No anxiety or skepticism present. John is a self-motivated, pre-researched customer — retired, flexible schedule, cooperative throughout. His risk profile is logistical (mobile lifestyle, not always near a computer) rather than emotional.
unresolved_concerns: John has a strong preference for a ground-mount solar field rather than rooftop panels, based on tree shading around his house. He has thought this through independently and will likely raise it with the closer. The closer should be prepared to engage this seriously — John described himself as 'mechanical' and will not be satisfied with a dismissive response.

John explicitly said he cannot guarantee he will not be driving at 4PM Friday. Show-rate is at risk. Consider a confirmation text or call Thursday to re-anchor the no-driving / at-computer requirement.
sensitive_topics_raised: John mentioned his family has owned the property since 1972 — long family history with the land. Treat the property with appropriate respect in the conversation.
exact_promises_rep_made: Closer will call John on Friday the 20th at 4PM.
Becky said she would get John 'all filled out with your estimated design' before Friday's call.
Becky texted John from her number during the call (bill photo exchange).
closer_should_know: John is a highly motivated, self-researching customer who has already mentally designed his own solar solution (ground-mount field, ~100 feet south of the house, away from tree shading). He will engage technically and will want the specialist to take his thinking seriously. His three stated motivations are saving money, ecology, and energy independence — but Becky never drew out which one is primary, so the closer should probe that early. The appointment is logistically fragile: John is mobile, declined the no-driving commitment, and the booking went through three time changes. A Thursday confirmation touch is recommended.
